homesEstimate.note: Estimated number of UK homes still served by lead supply pipes (either the service pipe or internal plumbing). Predominantly pre-1970 properties. Soft water areas have higher risk of leaching.

replacementRate.note: Lead service pipes replaced per year by water companies across England and Wales. Includes full and partial replacements.

yearsToComplete.note: Estimated years to replace all lead service pipes at the current annual replacement rate. Falls as rate accelerates.

sources.name: Ofwat
sources.dataset: PR24 Final Determinations — Lead Pipe Replacement Targets

sources.name: Drinking Water Inspectorate
sources.dataset: Drinking Water Quality in England and Wales

metadata.methodology: Home estimate from Water UK survey of company records and property age data. Replacement rates from Ofwat annual performance reporting. Years-to-complete calculated as (central estimate of 6 million homes) / (current annual replacement rate). PR24 target of 600,000/year from Ofwat final determinations.
knownIssues: The 6 million figure is an estimate — there is no national register of lead pipes and actual extent is uncertain
Partial lead pipe replacement (company-side only) may worsen lead leaching — WHO guidance recommends full replacement
Lead exposure risk varies greatly by water chemistry — soft water areas (Scotland, north-west England) have higher leaching rates
WHO revised lead guidelines in 2022 to state no safe level — UK standards under review
